title: Sports Widget — Implementation Spec
content: # Sports Widgets — Implementation Spec **One-liner:** Implement the visual redesign of `SportsWidget.tsx` per spec `e1a16c41` — fixing broken logos, horizontal overflow, missing game grouping, and hardcoded colors — without touching the data layer. --- ## Context The prior spec (`e1a16c41.md`) defined *what* to build. This spec translates that into *how* to build it, with precise file targets, component contracts, and a clear implementation checklist for the Lead Engineer and devs. **Affected files (only these should change):** - `src/components/widgets/SportsWidget.tsx` — full rewrite of `GameRow`, `SportsShell` - `src/components/widgets/SportsSettings.tsx` — no functional changes; CSS-var audit only - `src/components/widgets/registry.tsx` — no changes (sizes stay 340 × 420) - `src/hooks/useSports.ts` — **no changes** - `server/routes/sports.ts` — **no changes** --- ## User Stories 1. **As a user**, I see team logos (or a clean abbreviation badge fallback) so I can identify teams instantly without reading text. 2. **As a user**, I see games grouped into **Live**, **Upcoming**, and **Final** sections with clear headers so I know what's happening now vs. later. 3. **As a user**, I can resize the widget freely (min 240 px wide) and the layout scales cleanly — no horizontal scrollbar, no clipped text. 4. **As a user**, my favorite team's game is pinned to the top of its group and visually highlighted so I never have to hunt for it. --- ## Acceptance Criteria ### 1. Logo / Fallback Badge - [ ] Every `` for a team logo has `crossOrigin="anonymous"` and an `onError` handler that swaps to a fallback badge. - [ ] Fallback badge: circular `` showing team abbreviation (≤ 3 chars), background `var(--wt-surface-raised)`, text `var(--wt-text-muted)`, no broken-image icon ever shown. - [ ] Logo size: `clamp(18px, 6cqw, 32px)` (or equivalent `ResizeObserver`-driven value). - [ ] Logo `` has `alt="{team.abbr} logo"`; fallback badge has `aria-label="{team.abbr}"`. ### 2. Game Grouping - [ ] Games are rendered in three labeled sections in order: **Live** → **Upcoming** → **Final**. - [ ] Sections with zero games are hidden entirely (no empty header rendered). - [ ] Section header style: `var(--wt-text-muted)`, `text-transform: uppercase`, `font-size: clamp(9px, 2.5cqw, 11px)`, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`. - [ ] Live section header includes a pulsing dot using `var(--wt-danger)` (fallback `#ef4444`) and the existing `pulse` CSS animation. - [ ] Favorite team's game is sorted to the top of whichever section it belongs to. ### 3. Overflow / Responsive Layout - [ ] No horizontal scrollbar at any widget width ≥ 240 px. - [ ] No vertical scrollbar at default size (340 × 420) when ≤ 8 games are present. Vertical scroll is allowed when game count genuinely exceeds container height. - [ ] `SportsShell` uses `ResizeObserver` (same pattern as `WeatherWidget`) to derive a `containerWidth` value; font sizes and padding scale from it. - [ ] At `containerWidth < 280`: hide win/loss records; show only abbr + score. - [ ] At `containerWidth ≥ 280`: show records on pre-game rows. - [ ] All text nodes use `overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap` — no wrapping. ### 4. Colors & Theming - [ ] Zero hardcoded color values anywhere in `SportsWidget.tsx` or `SportsSettings.tsx`. - [ ] Allowed CSS vars: `var(--wt-accent)`, `var(--wt-text)`, `var(--wt-text-muted)`, `var(--wt-border)`, `var(--wt-surface)`, `var(--wt-surface-raised)`, `var(--wt-danger)`. - [ ] Favorite team highlight: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--wt-accent) 10%, transparent)` background + `2px solid var(--wt-accent)` left border (existing pattern, just de-hardcode). - [ ] Live score text color: `var(--wt-danger)`. ### 5. Icons - [ ] All non-logo icons (live dot, any chevrons) use Phosphor icons via the existing `` component — no raw SVG or emoji. ### 6. Accessibility - [ ] Game list container: `role="list"`. - [ ] Each `GameRow`: `role="listitem"`. - [ ] Live score/status text: wrapped in ``. - [ ] Section headers: `role="heading" aria-level={3}`. ### 7. Component Contract - [ ] `SportsShell` accepts `{ league: League; settings: SportsWidgetSettings }` — unchanged signature. - [ ] `GameRow` accepts `{ game: Game; favoriteTeam: string; containerWidth: number }` — `containerWidth` added to drive responsive hiding. - [ ] `NFLWidget` and `NBAWidget` export signatures unchanged. - [ ] `SportsSettings`, `NFLSettings`, `NBASettings` export signatures unchanged. ### 8. Registry - [ ] `registry.tsx` is **not modified** — default sizes remain `{ width: 340, height: 420 }` for both NFL and NBA. ### 9. TypeScript - [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors after changes. - [ ] No `any` types introduced in `SportsWidget.tsx`. ### 10. No Data Layer Changes - [ ] `useSports.ts` is **not modified**. - [ ] `server/routes/sports.ts` is **not modified**. --- ## Default Size - **Width:** 340 px - **Height:** 420 px *(unchanged — must look correct at this size with no scroll for a typical game day)* ## Settings / Preferences - **Favorite Team** (existing, string) — team abbreviation; game pinned to top of its section and highlighted. - No new settings introduced in this implementation. ## Data Sources - ESPN Scoreboard API via `/api/sports/:league` (proxied by `server/routes/sports.ts`) - Polled every 60 s via `useNFLScores` / `useNBAScores` (unchanged) - Supported leagues in server: `nfl`, `nba`, `premierleague`, `laliga`, `ucl`, `mlb`, `nhl` — only `nfl` and `nba` are wired to widgets in this task. --- ## Implementation Notes for Lead Engineer > These are architectural nudges — defer to Lead on final approach. 1. **ResizeObserver pattern** — copy the `useResizeObserver` usage from `WeatherWidget.tsx` verbatim. Attach to the root `div` ref; store `containerWidth` in state; pass down to `GameRow`. 2. **Logo CORS** — `crossOrigin="anonymous"` + `onError` fallback badge is the agreed approach. Server-side logo proxying is **out of scope** for this task (see Open Questions). 3. **`SportsShell` refactor** — keep it in `SportsWidget.tsx` for now; extraction to a `sports/` subdirectory is a separate task (see Open Questions). 4. **`var(--wt-danger)` availability** — confirm this var exists in the theme before using; if not, use `#ef4444` as the only permitted hardcoded color exception. 5. **Pulse animation** — reuse the existing `@keyframes pulse` already defined in the global stylesheet; do not add a new one. --- ## Out of Scope - Adding new league widgets (MLB, NHL, Premier League, La Liga, UCL) — server routes exist but widget registration is a separate task. - Server-side logo image proxying. - Click-through to game detail pages. - Push notifications for score changes. - Dark/light mode toggle (handled globally by CSS vars). - Any functional changes to `SportsSettings.tsx`. - Performance optimization of the ESPN API proxy. - Extraction of `SportsShell` to a `sports/` subdirectory. --- ## Open Questions 1. **Logo CORS** — ESPN logo URLs (`a.espncdn.com`) may block cross-origin requests even with `crossOrigin="anonymous"`. If the fallback badge fires on every load, should the server proxy logo images? *(Defer to Lead Engineer — architecture/reliability call.)* 2. **`SportsShell` location** — Keep in `SportsWidget.tsx` or extract to `src/components/widgets/sports/SportsShell.tsx` in anticipation of MLB/NHL? *(Defer to Lead Engineer.)* 3. **`var(--wt-danger)` token** — Does this CSS var exist in the current theme? If not, what is the correct token for red/error states? *(Lead or Dev 2 to confirm before implementation.)* 4. **Vertical scroll at short heights** — If user resizes widget to a very short height, show a "X more games" collapsed state or just allow scroll? *(Default: allow scroll, no collapse — confirm with founder if needed.)* 5. **`containerWidth` prop vs. CSS container queries** — `ResizeObserver` state is the established pattern (WeatherWidget), but CSS container queries (`cqw`) would be cleaner. Use `ResizeObserver` for consistency unless Lead prefers container queries. *(Defer to Lead.)*
